reforms.description: The Hungarian government plans to strengthen the role of the National Competitiveness Council (NCC) to improve productivity and competitiveness through independent analysis and policy advice.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Strengthening the NCC is expected to enhance productivity and competitiveness, which supports economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The reform has been officially announced but not yet legislated.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is a minor adjustment to existing governance structures.
reforms.source_quote: The role of this institution to analyse productivity developments could be strengthened and made more visible.

reforms.description: The Family Housing Support Programme provides preferential loans and grants for families with children to support homeownership and modernisation of dwellings, with a budgetary cost of approximately 1.1% of GDP from 2019-2022.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: This programme aims to enhance homeownership and modernisation, contributing to economic stability and growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The programme has been officially announced but not yet implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: While significant, this reform is an extension of existing support mechanisms.
reforms.source_quote: The Family Housing Support Programme provides preferential loans and grants for families with children, supporting homeownership and modernisation of dwellings.

reforms.description: The Warm Homes Programme aims to provide non-refundable grants to improve the energy efficiency of houses and assist the development of renewable energy, having funded the modernisation of 210,000 family dwellings.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improving energy efficiency contributes to sustainability and economic resilience.
reforms.status_evidence: The programme has been officially announced but not yet implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative is significant but builds on existing energy efficiency efforts.
reforms.source_quote: The Warm Homes Programme aims to provide non-refundable grants to improve the energy efficiency of houses and assist the development of renewable energy.

reforms.description: A new regulation, operational from November 2023, will require energy performance certificates for all newly constructed, sold, and rented buildings, along with sanctions for non-compliance, aiming to close the information gap on energy efficiency and provide better incentives for housing renovations.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: This regulation is expected to enhance knowledge and incentives for energy efficiency improvements.
reforms.status_evidence: The regulation is operational and has been implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is significant as it introduces new regulatory requirements.
reforms.source_quote: A new regulation, operational from November 2023, will see energy performance certificates apply to all newly constructed, sold and rented buildings with sanctions for non-compliance.

reforms.description: The Green Bus Programme aims to reduce emissions from local public buses by subsidizing the purchase of electric buses and the development of necessary charging infrastructure, with 139 electric buses acquired as of 2023, significantly contributing to urban air quality improvements.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: This initiative supports environmental sustainability and urban mobility improvements.
reforms.status_evidence: The programme has been implemented with electric buses acquired.
reforms.importance_rationale: This programme is significant but part of ongoing efforts to improve public transport.
reforms.source_quote: The Green Bus Programme can lead to lower emissions from local public buses and cut air pollution in urban areas.

reforms.description: Hungary is planning to become a leading battery producer through significant investments in battery production for electric cars, including a planned 100 GWh battery plant in Debrecen, which would be Europe's largest electric car battery factory.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: This initiative is expected to significantly boost the economy by positioning Hungary as a key player in the electric vehicle supply chain.
reforms.status_evidence: The initiative has been officially announced but not yet implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a major strategic investment in a key sector.
reforms.source_quote: Hungary is planning to become a leading battery producer through a number of large-scale investments in battery production for electric cars.

reforms.description: Hungary's renewable energy support scheme (METÁR) was established to promote the expansion of renewable energy sources, combining feed-in tariffs and premiums for small and mid-size energy plants, with plans for competitive bidding for larger plants.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: This scheme encourages investment in renewable energy, supporting sustainable economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The scheme has been implemented and is operational.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is significant but builds on existing renewable energy policies.
reforms.source_quote: The main measure for expanding renewables is the renewable energy support scheme (METÁR) of 2017, which combines feed-in tariffs and feed-in premiums for small and mid-size energy plants.

reforms.description: The Hungarian government proposed de-risking plans to facilitate the development of geothermal energy, which includes a regulatory change to simplify the licensing system for geothermal exploration and production, leading to a substantial increase in geothermal projects with 89 new permits since March 2023.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Facilitating geothermal energy development supports sustainable energy production and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The de-risking plans have been implemented, resulting in new permits.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is significant but part of broader energy policy efforts.
reforms.source_quote: The March 2023 regulatory change to the Act on Mining simplified the licensing system for geothermal exploration and production of energy.
